title: #019: Levi Strauss's Earnings, Walmart Links Up w/ ChatGPT, and Instacart's Partnership w/ TikTok
description:
In today's episode:
Ashley covers OpenAI's recent integration of in-app product recommendations from Walmart Garrett breaks down Levi Strauss's earnings call and why it shows a disconnect between performance and predictionAshley breaks down Instacart and TikTok's audience data sharing and talks through how she thinks it'll affect retail media

title: May 12th, 2025: Shopify Needs Incentive Alignment to Win in Enterprise, 3 Notables From BigCommerce earnings call, Instacart CEO Departs for OpenAI, and Shopify Reports Solid Earnings Amid Global Uncertainty
show: The Watson Weekly: eCommerce Strategy & News
description:
In this week’s Watson Weekly, we will discuss Fidji Simo is out at Instacart CEO to join OpenAI.  This is a huge feather in Simo’s cap and leaves an opening at Instacart which I predict will be filled by their Chief Product Officer Daniel Danker.  Not only did Danker and Simo overlap from their time at Facebook, but Danker was promoted by Simo into his current role as Chief Product Officer.

title: Ty Simpson’s Draft stock, Jaxon Smith-Njigba’s extension + Ladd McConkey bounce-back?
description: Patrick Daugherty (@RotoPat) and Denny Carter break down all the latest NFL news, beginning with the scuttlebutt over Alabama QB Ty Simpson’s draft status. Is he actually a first-round talent, and would he be a disastrous fit with the Jets, for whom he will work out? They then ponder Jaxon Smith-Njigba’s ADP following his record-setting extension, Ladd McConkey’s bounce-back odds and much more. That includes Zay Flowers’ ceiling or lack thereof, Jauan Jennings and Stefon Diggs’ quiet free agency

title: You Can't Sell What You Can't Afford to Stock | Scaling Zea from Market Stalls to Woolworths | #603
show: Add To Cart: Australia’s eCommerce Show
description:
Building an ecommerce brand often starts with a great product and a loyal customer base. But scaling that brand introduces a whole new set of challenges, especially when growth starts to outpace cashflow.
In this episode of Add To Cart, Bushy sits down with Hayden Brass, founder of Zea, alongside David Carbines, co-founder of Cogsflow, to unpack the financial realities behind scaling a product brand.
Today, we're discussing:
